private_memo:
# Morning memo — 2026-06-17

**Source failures (if any):** None observed.

## Worth attention

- **Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to US government policy clash** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/axios-clashes-anthropics/#atom-everything
  An Axios investigation (via Simon Willison) reports that Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline after a clash with the White House over the 'Fable 5' jailbreak and export control designation. Sources close to Anthropic say 'they screwed us.' For any developer running Claude-dependent workflows (including second-brain, multi-agent setups), this is a concrete reminder that API availability is not guaranteed during policy disputes. Consider having a multi-provider fallback.

- **A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer** — https://roman.pt/posts/linkedin-backdoor/
  A security researcher documented a supply chain attack: a polished LinkedIn job offer directs the candidate to clone and run a GitHub repo that installs a persistent backdoor while appearing legitimate. The attack passes casual code review. Any solo developer who evaluates contractor code or does take-home tests should be aware of this vector and run unknown repos in sandboxes.

- **datasette-agent 0.3a0: execute_write_sql with user approval** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/datasette-agent/#atom-everything
  Simon Willison's datasette-agent MCP gains an `execute_write_sql` tool that requests user approval before writing to the database. This is a concrete, working implementation of the 'dangerous action requires human confirmation' pattern for agent workflows — directly applicable if you're building MCP tools that touch databases.

- **Cloudflare CAPTCHA triggered by URLs containing ampersands** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/captcha-on-at-least-one-ampersand/#atom-everything
  TIL from Simon Willison: Cloudflare WAF Managed Challenge rules can trigger CAPTCHA on requests with literal ampersands in the URL, breaking faceted search engines. Workaround: URL-encode ampersands or scope WAF rules more tightly. Easy to miss and hard to debug without knowing the cause.

- **Vercel Functions now support up to 30-minute execution** — https://vercel.com/changelog/vercel-functions-can-now-run-up-to-30-minutes
  Pro and Enterprise Vercel Functions (Node.js and Python) can now run up to 30 minutes, more than doubling the previous ~13-minute cap. Relevant for AI pipeline functions, document processing, or anything that previously required a separate queue/worker setup to handle the time limit.

- **Iroh 1.0: Dial Keys, not IPs** — https://www.iroh.computer/blog/v1
  Iroh, a Rust-based P2P networking library, hit v1.0. Uses public keys instead of IP addresses for connections, with NAT traversal and relay fallback. API is now stable. Worth tracking for agent-to-agent networking or building tools that need direct device connections without centralized infrastructure.

- **Typst 0.15 released** — https://typst.app/blog/2026/typst-0.15/
  Typst 0.15, the modern LaTeX alternative, released with significant new features. Worth checking if you do any technical document generation or templated PDF production.

## Full digest
- [P] [simonwillison] Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to government clash — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/axios-clashes-anthropics/ — API outage driven by Fable 5 jailbreak/export control conflict; plan for Claude API unavailability
- [P] [hacker-news] A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer — https://roman.pt/posts/linkedin-backdoor/ — Supply chain attack via polished job offer repo; installs persistent backdoor on clone+run
- [P] [simonwillison] datasette-agent 0.3a0 — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/datasette-agent/ — execute_write_sql with user approval — human-in-the-loop pattern for agent DB writes
- [P] [simonwillison] Cloudflare CAPTCHA on URLs with ampersands — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/captcha-on-at-least-one-ampersand/ — WAF Managed Challenge triggers on & in URLs; fix: URL-encode or scope rules
- [P] [vercel-changelog] Vercel Functions now up to 30 min execution — https://vercel.com/changelog/vercel-functions-can-now-run-up-to-30-minutes — Pro/Enterprise Node.js/Python; 2x previous cap
- [M] [iroh] Iroh 1.0 — https://www.iroh.computer/blog/v1 — Stable v1.0 Rust P2P library; keys not IPs, NAT traversal
- [P] [hacker-news] Typst 0.15 — https://typst.app/blog/2026/typst-0.15/ — Major release of modern document typesetting tool
- [R] [gh-nextjs] v16.3.0-canary.52 — https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v16.3.0-canary.52 — Canary; rspack 2.0 + experimental Turbopack React compiler
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Workflow SDK inflight cancellation — https://vercel.com/changelog/workflow-sdk-now-supports-inflight-cancellation — Beta, Vercel-specific
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Workflow SDK TanStack Start — https://vercel.com/changelog/workflow-sdk-now-supports-tanstack-start — Too niche
- [R] [latent.space] AINews Satya on Loopcraft — https://www.latent.space/p/ainews-satya-on-loopcraft-building — Editorial newsletter, no actionable claim
- [R] [simonwillison] Fable 5 Export Controls Harm US Cyber Defense — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/fable-5-export-controls/ — Policy commentary; covered by the main outage story
- [R] [simonwillison] Quoting Matteo Wong / Atlantic on Fable — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/matteo-wong-the-atlantic/ — Duplicate angle on outage story
- [R] [simonwillison] x86 emulator team found code so bad they fixed it during emulation — https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260615-00/?p=112419 — Historical tech story; not actionable
- [R] [simonwillison] John Carmack on Fabrice Bellard — https://twitter.com/ID_AA_Carmack/status/2064095424420487226 — Social media link, no content
- [R] [hacker-news] Banned Book Library in a WiFi Light Bulb — https://www.richardosgood.com/posts/banned-book-library/ — Creative hack; not actionable
- [R] [hacker-news] Garden of Flowers typography archive — https://garden-of-flowers.heikkilotvonen.com/ — Historical archive; not actionable
- [R] [hacker-news] FreeBSD 15 on a Laptop — https://www.sacredheartsc.com/blog/freebsd-15-on-a-laptop/ — OS report; not relevant to stack
- [R] [hacker-news] Iroh 1.0 HN discussion — https://www.iroh.computer/blog/v1 — Duplicate of Iroh 1.0 blog post

results.title: Next.js v16.3.0-canary.52
results.summary: Next.js canary release upgrading to rspack 2.0 and adding experimental React compiler support for Turbopack. Pre-release only; rspack bump is maintenance-level, Turbopack React compiler is experimental.
results.raw_claim: Next.js v16.3.0-canary.52 upgrades next-rspack to rspack 2.0 and adds experimental React compiler support for Turbopack.
results.uncertainty: Canary builds are unstable; changes may not land in stable as described.
results.verdict_reason: Canary pre-release; no stable user-facing features — dependency bump only.

results.title: Workflow SDK now supports inflight cancellation
results.summary: Vercel Workflow SDK 5 beta adds AbortController/AbortSignal support across workflow and step boundaries, allowing cancellation of in-flight operations that survive suspensions and deterministic replay. Beta, Vercel-specific.
results.raw_claim: Vercel Workflow SDK 5 beta now supports AbortController/AbortSignal for cancelling in-flight workflow steps across suspension boundaries.
results.uncertainty: Beta product; API may change before stable release.
results.verdict_reason: Beta, Vercel platform-specific, not broadly applicable.

results.title: Workflow SDK now supports TanStack Start
results.summary: Vercel Workflow SDK now supports TanStack Start apps on Vercel using the existing Vite/Nitro plugin. Niche: requires both TanStack Start and the Vercel platform.
results.raw_claim: Vercel Workflow SDK now supports TanStack Start applications with durable, resumable workflow functions via the workflow/vite plugin.
results.uncertainty: TanStack Start itself is still relatively new.
results.verdict_reason: Too niche — requires TanStack Start on Vercel.

results.title: Vercel Functions can now run up to 30 minutes
results.summary: Vercel Pro and Enterprise Functions (Node.js and Python runtimes) now support up to 30-minute execution durations, more than doubling the previous ~13-minute (800s) cap. Relevant for AI pipeline functions, batch processing, or document generation tasks that previously required a separate queue/worker architecture. Other runtimes coming soon.
results.raw_claim: Vercel Pro/Enterprise Functions can now run up to 30 minutes for Node.js and Python runtimes, up from the previous 800-second limit.
results.uncertainty: Pro/Enterprise only; other runtimes not yet supported.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Changes the calculus for long-running tasks on Vercel — previously you'd need a queue/worker setup to stay under the old limit.
results.verdict_reason: Meaningful platform limit increase for Vercel users; reduces need for separate worker infrastructure for long AI tasks.

results.title: [AINews] Satya on Loopcraft: Building Frontier Ecosystems
results.summary: A latent.space AI newsletter issue covering Satya Nadella's 'Loopcraft' essay about building frontier AI ecosystems. Editorial commentary on a quiet news day; no actionable claim.
results.raw_claim: AI News newsletter covers Satya Nadella's Loopcraft essay about building frontier AI ecosystems.
results.uncertainty: Secondary source; unclear what the essay argues without reading it.
results.verdict_reason: Editorial commentary on an essay; no actionable claim.

results.title: The Fable 5 Export Controls Harm US Cyber Defense
results.summary: Simon Willison quotes Kate Moussouris (Luta Security) on the Fable 5 jailbreak situation: the White House classified Claude as a potential weapons-enabling tool, Anthropic is pushing back. Policy commentary with no independent actionable claim beyond the main model-offline story.
results.raw_claim: Cybersecurity expert Kate Moussouris argues that applying export controls to AI models due to the Fable 5 jailbreak harms US cyber defense capabilities.
results.uncertainty: Policy situation is evolving; outcome unclear.
results.verdict_reason: Policy commentary; covered better by the main Anthropic offline story.

results.title: Anthropic/Claude and the Fable 5 export control situation — The Atlantic
results.summary: Simon Willison quotes The Atlantic's Matteo Wong on the Fable jailbreak: cybersecurity expert Moussouris confirmed the jailbreak was genuine but argues export controls are the wrong response. Duplicate angle on the main Anthropic outage story.
results.raw_claim: Katie Moussouris confirmed the Fable jailbreak of Claude was real but argues that applying export controls as a result harms US cyber defense.
results.uncertainty: Attribution via anonymous sources; Anthropic not commenting publicly.
results.verdict_reason: Duplicate of the main Anthropic outage story; no additional actionable information.

results.title: Cloudflare CAPTCHA triggered by URLs containing ampersands
results.summary: Simon Willison discovered that Cloudflare's WAF Managed Challenge rules can trigger a CAPTCHA on requests with literal ampersands (&) in the URL, breaking faceted search query strings. Workaround: URL-encode ampersands or scope WAF rules more tightly. Easy to miss; hard to debug without knowing the root cause.
results.raw_claim: Cloudflare WAF Managed Challenge rules can trigger CAPTCHA on URLs containing literal ampersands, breaking faceted search engines and similar query-string-heavy pages.
results.uncertainty: May depend on specific WAF rule configuration; not necessarily universal across all Cloudflare setups.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Practical gotcha that would be hard to debug without knowing the cause — saves hours of debugging time.
results.verdict_reason: Practical debugging tip for Cloudflare WAF users; saves significant debugging time.

results.title: datasette-agent 0.3a0: execute_write_sql with user approval
results.summary: Simon Willison released datasette-agent 0.3a0, adding an `execute_write_sql` MCP tool that presents a write query to the user for approval before executing it against the database. This is a concrete working implementation of the 'dangerous action requires human confirmation' pattern for AI agent workflows — directly applicable to MCP server design.
results.raw_claim: datasette-agent 0.3a0 adds execute_write_sql tool that requests user approval before writing to the database, implementing human-in-the-loop for agent DB writes.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Human approval pattern for agent DB writes is directly applicable to MCP server design for agentic workflows.
results.verdict_reason: Concrete agent workflow pattern (human approval for writes) from a credible practitioner; directly relevant to MCP work.

results.title: Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to US government policy clash over Fable jailbreak
results.summary: An Axios investigation (via Simon Willison) reports that Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline after conflict with the White House over the 'Fable 5' jailbreak and export control designation. Sources close to Anthropic describe the shutdown as retaliatory, saying 'they screwed us.' For any developer running Claude-dependent workflows or agents, this is a concrete reminder that API availability is not guaranteed during policy disputes — worth having a multi-provider fallback strategy.
results.raw_claim: Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline due to conflict with the US government over the Fable 5 jailbreak and export control classification.
results.uncertainty: Based on anonymous sources cited in Axios; Anthropic has not made official statements. Exact timeline and restoration status unclear.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Direct impact on Claude API availability — affects any builder running Claude-dependent automation or agents.
results.verdict_reason: Direct impact on Claude API reliability; any builder dependent on Claude should know models can go offline due to policy disputes.

results.title: The time the x86 emulator team found code so bad they fixed it during emulation
results.summary: Raymond Chen (Old New Thing) recounts a story from Microsoft's x86-on-ARM emulation team finding application code so broken they implemented runtime patches in the emulator itself. Historical tech story with no actionable implications for current projects.
results.raw_claim: Microsoft's Windows-on-ARM x86 emulation team implemented runtime patches for application code too broken to run without fixes during emulation.
results.verdict_reason: Historical tech story; entertaining but not actionable.

results.title: John Carmack on Fabrice Bellard
results.summary: A link to a John Carmack tweet expressing admiration for Fabrice Bellard. No substantive content beyond a social media post.
results.raw_claim: John Carmack posted on X expressing admiration for Fabrice Bellard.
results.uncertainty: Tweet content not available without accessing X.
results.verdict_reason: Social media link with no substantive content.

results.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
results.summary: A security researcher documents a supply chain attack delivered via LinkedIn: an attacker sends a polished job offer with a GitHub repo to clone and run. The code installs a persistent backdoor while appearing to be a legitimate coding exercise. The attack passes casual code review. Any developer who evaluates contractor code, open-source contributions, or take-home tests should sandbox unknown repos.
results.raw_claim: A security researcher documented a supply chain attack where a LinkedIn job offer directed developers to clone a GitHub repo that installed a persistent backdoor.
results.uncertainty: Specifics of the backdoor mechanism depend on reading the full post.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Real documented attack vector targeting developers via LinkedIn — high risk for solo devs who review code for freelance or contract work.
results.verdict_reason: Documented real-world attack targeting developers via LinkedIn job offers; directly actionable — sandbox unknown repos.

results.title: Banned Book Library in a Wi-Fi Smart Light Bulb
results.summary: A creative project embedding a library of banned books in a smart light bulb's WiFi captive portal. Clever hardware hack but no practical application for solo builder workflows.
results.raw_claim: A developer embedded a banned book library accessible via captive WiFi portal in a smart light bulb.
results.verdict_reason: Creative hardware hack; not actionable for solo builder work.

results.title: Iroh 1.0: Dial Keys, not IPs
results.summary: Iroh, a Rust-based peer-to-peer networking library, has reached v1.0. It enables direct encrypted connections between devices using public keys instead of IP addresses, with NAT traversal and relay fallback. API is now stable. Potentially useful for agent-to-agent networking or building tools that need device-to-device connections without centralized infrastructure.
results.raw_claim: Iroh 1.0 released — a stable Rust P2P networking library that uses public keys instead of IPs for connections, with NAT traversal and relay fallback.
results.uncertainty: Rust-only for now; ecosystem adoption unclear.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Stable 1.0 P2P library for direct device-to-device connections without centralized infrastructure.
results.verdict_reason: Notable v1.0 P2P library with stable API; worth tracking for agent networking use cases.

results.title: Garden of Flowers: archive of pictorial typography before ASCII art
results.summary: A historical archive of text-based visual art predating ASCII art, created from a 2015 BA thesis on Amiga ASCII art. Interesting historical artifact; not actionable for solo builder work.
results.raw_claim: A developer published an archive of pre-ASCII pictorial typography based on their 2015 BA thesis research on Amiga ASCII art.
results.verdict_reason: Historical archive; not actionable for solo builder.

results.title: FreeBSD 15 on a Laptop
results.summary: An experience report of running FreeBSD 15 as a laptop daily driver. Not relevant to Fuzzy's macOS/Linux/Hetzner stack.
results.raw_claim: A developer published their experience running FreeBSD 15 as a laptop daily driver.
results.verdict_reason: OS experience report not relevant to current stack.

results.title: Iroh 1.0 — Hacker News discussion
results.summary: Hacker News discussion thread for the Iroh 1.0 release blog post. Same underlying story as item 016532f2; no additional content warranting a separate entry.
results.raw_claim: Hacker News discussion of the Iroh 1.0 P2P networking library release.
results.verdict_reason: Duplicate — same story as Iroh 1.0 blog post; HN discussion adds no new content.

results.title: Typst 0.15 released
results.summary: Typst 0.15, the modern LaTeX-alternative document typesetting system, released with significant new features (HN title 'contains multitudes' signals a feature-rich release). Worth checking if you do any technical document generation, academic writing, or templated PDF production — the tool is maturing rapidly.
results.raw_claim: Typst 0.15 released with significant new features in the modern LaTeX-alternative document typesetting system.
results.uncertainty: HN link is to comments; full feature list requires reading the official blog post.
results.worth_mentioning_reason: Notable release for document generation tooling; worth checking for PDF/report generation workflows.
results.verdict_reason: Notable release of a useful document generation tool; low effort to evaluate.
